titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5964

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=768 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.683294+00 | translation moa=769 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.691044+00

p.cl: . Omnibus hominibus hoc scriptum visuris uel audituris Eustacius de Nubiging salutem .

Nouerint
vniuersitas nostra me Ratam et Gratam habere . Illam vendicionem quam Willelmus de Scremerstona
fecit Domino . R . Priori et Conuentui de Coldingham de omnibus terriis quas Idem Willelmus habuit
ex dono et vendicione Roberti de Prendergest’ in villa et In territorio de prendergest . Ita videlicet quod nec
ego nec aliquis per me uel pro me Ius aut clamium nullo tempore seculi in predictus terris
possimus vendicare .
In Cuius Rei Testimonium  presenti scripto sigillum meum apposui .

Hiis Test
ibus . Domino Willelmo de lindesey . Domino Willelmo de Morington . Ada de prendergest . Ada filio Iohannis de superiori ayton .
Bertramo de Riston’ . Patricio scotto . Roberto filio Gregorii de Coldingham . Gilberto de lumesden Dauid de lume
sden . Willelmo filio Waldui de Riston’ . Rogero filio prepositi . et Aliis .

div.p: To all men who shall view or hear this writing, Eustace of Newbiggin sends greeting. Know me, all of you, to have ratified the sale which William of Scremerston made to the lord prior, Richard, and the convent of Coldingham, of all the lands which the same William had out of Robert of Prenderguest's gift and sale, in the toun and territory of Prenderguest. So that neither I nor anyone through me or for me in any earthly age should be able to sell the right or claim. In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to the present writing. With these witnesses: the lord William of Lindsay, the lord William of Mordington, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Over Ayton, Bertram of Reston, Patrick Scott, Robert son of Gregory of Coldingham, Gilbert of Lumsdaine, David of Lumsdaine, William son of Waldef of Reston, Roger son of the grieve, and others.
